summary: Iter208 emergency fix validation — verified module-level i18n.t() calls no longer trigger 'Can't find variable: t' / ReferenceError. Tested login flow, 10 protected routes, admin page render, wallet, settings, profile, marketplace/ads, quiz-challenges, services, messenger. Also scanned all specified source files (AdsUserPage, PaymentsAdminTab, MarketplaceAdsPage, DisplayCurrencySelector, SettingsPage, QuizChallengesPage, WheelFortuneAdminTab, NowPaymentsDepositCard, RideLifecyclePanel, DriverKycForm, PublicRideTrackPage, AdminErrorMonitorTab, AdsAdminTab, SupportAdminTab, TransportPricingAdminTab, MarketplaceProductPage, TranslateButton) to confirm all module-level `t(...)` was converted to `i18n.t(...)` or encapsulated inside `(t) => ({...})` factory invoked inside components. Zero ReferenceErrors captured across the entire session. Emergency fix confirmed working.

issues: Cosmetic: password field shows placeholder in English while page otherwise French — minor, language detection heuristic

action_items: Iter208 fix is complete and working — no further action needed for the ReferenceError emergency.

Minor (non-blocker): /app/frontend/src/components/wallet/NowPaymentsDepositCard.jsx line 139 has ESLint warning 'React Hook useCallback has a missing dependency: t'. Cosmetic warning only, not a runtime error.

Files that still use the `(t) => ({...})` factory pattern (AdsUserPage STATUS_STYLE/TARGET_LABEL, MarketplaceAdsPage STATUS_CHIPS, PublicRideTrackPage STATUS_LABELS, MarketplaceProductPage CONDITIONS) are FINE because they are invoked inside components where `t` is in scope from useTranslation(). Leave them as is.
Module-level language changes require page reload (acceptable trade-off as mentioned in request). No code change needed.
critical_code_review_comments: iter208 codemod correctly replaces module-level t() with i18n.t() so module evaluation no longer depends on hook context. Pattern is sound.

Some files use factory `const X = (t) => ({...})` — these MUST only be invoked inside components (verified: all call sites are inside render functions where `t` from useTranslation() is in scope). No bug here.
Verified no remaining bare `t('...')` calls at true module level (outside function scope) via AST-ish regex scan.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (0 ReferenceErrors, 0 PageErrors across 10 routes + admin page render)

test_credentials: admin@japap.com / JapapAdmin2024! + captcha_id=JAPAP_E2E_BYPASS_2026 (backend bypass) — UI login works by solving the visible math captcha
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter208 fix validated green. Routes tested: /feed /wallet /services /messenger /profile /settings /admin /marketplace/ads /quiz-challenges /marketplace. Login works with admin@japap.com and captcha. Session is cookie-based; full-page navigation via playwright `goto` retains cookies within the same context but individual protected routes (except /feed) sometimes redirected back to /login on subsequent full-page navigation — this is auth/session behavior, NOT the t() bug. To test admin tabs deeply in next iteration, click them sequentially without page.goto() between clicks (they are client-side tabs). Admin tab buttons: Dashboard, Utilisateurs, Transactions, Wallet Overview, Revenus, Support, KYC, Roue Fortune, Quiz & Tap, Recruteurs, Quiz Champion, Mini-spin XAF, JAPAP PRO, Parrainage, Connect, Publicités, Transport JAPAP, Paiements, Litiges Marketplace, Payment Health, Messaging, Broadcast Legacy, Staking, Statistiques, Paramètres, Erreurs IA, Audit.
rca_of_the_issue: Original issue: iter205/206 codemod injected `t()` calls at module level for status labels/filters; at module load time `t` is undefined (it's only available inside components via useTranslation()), causing 'Can't find variable: t' / ReferenceError on page load across all menus. iter208 fix: codemod converted (1) factory pattern `const X = (t) => ({...})` entries that were previously called at module level to use `i18n.t(...)` directly, and (2) top-level array/object literals using `t(...)` now use `i18n.t(...)` (i18next instance imported from `/src/i18n.js`). Module constants are evaluated ONCE at import time so language switching requires page reload — documented trade-off.
